claim: The five registered RCCD components are jointly non-redundant relative to the frozen class, preservation contract, admissible representation universe, machinery closure, commitment equivalence, and component necessity lemmas.

independence_standard.individual: For every component X there exists a closed admissible separating witness preserving the other four component obligations while losing at least one obligation distinctive of X.
independence_standard.pairwise: No component may be identified with another by renaming, shared storage, shared outputs, or an unproved one-way translation.
independence_standard.joint: No component may be derived from the union of the other four unless an effective total non-circular equivalence-invariant construction recovers every frozen obligation and accounts for all machinery.
independence_standard.interaction: Independence does not imply isolation; cross-component constraints and shared machinery remain permitted when their distinct preservation obligations remain separately recoverable.

separating_witnesses.example: A fully typed interpreted transition trace whose current commitments cannot be queried or stably identified.
A commitment-bearing interpreted dependency history in which every candidate change is accepted.
A semantically interpreted commitment revision log with no recoverable support, defeat, or reason relations.
A stable commitment-transition dependency trace over opaque identifiers with no recoverable content conditions.
A semantically interpreted commitment and dependency snapshot with admissible changes but no recoverable event lineage.

anti_trivialization: different names do not prove independence
shared implementation does not prove identity
correlated outputs do not prove reduction
one-way simulation does not prove recoverability
post hoc human explanation is not executable reduction
a reduction may not invoke the target component under another name
unknown evidence is not promoted to independence or reducibility
unproved: irreducibility against every possible alternative vocabulary
